size_shuffled.status: UNCHANGED — shuffling size within domains has no effect

no_zero_padding.status: SURVIVES — even stronger without padding artifacts

verdict.key_finding: Rank-normalization STRENGTHENS PC1 from 17.6% to 38.2%. This means the axis is driven by RANK ORDER across domains, not by raw scale values. Removing the explicit size feature does not kill it. Shuffling within domains does not change it. Removing zero-padding strengthens it.
verdict.strongest_evidence: Rank-normalization should destroy a pure scale artifact. Instead it strengthens the axis. This suggests Megethos captures ORDINAL structure, not just magnitude.
verdict.remaining_risk: The shuffle control (C3) shows PC1 is UNCHANGED when size is shuffled — meaning PC1 is NOT driven by the size feature at all in raw space. The original PC1 loadings show features 2,1,3 dominating, not feature 0. Megethos in phoneme space may be a different object than PC1 in raw space.
